query: Recent PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan ADIZ in May-June 2026, and reported pause or drop in activity. What are typical daily counts recently and highest single-day count in 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**On the "pause/drop":** The dramatic pause in PLA air activity occurred in **February–March 2026** (roughly Feb 27–Mar 12, ~2 weeks of near-zero aircraft), coinciding with China's "Two Sessions" — **not** in May–June. May–June 2026 continued a subdued but steady pattern of activity at multi-year lows.

**Typical daily counts (May–June 2026):** Single digits to low-mid double digits. Sampled MND daily reports: May 17 = 5, May 27 = 10, June 13 = 6, June 26 = 2, July 2 = 22. June's monthly total (~214 sorties) implies roughly ~7 sorties/day on average, with many near-zero days.

**Highest single-day count in 2026:** **36 aircraft on March 17, 2026** — no day in April, May, or June 2026 exceeded 30 sorties.

**Monthly context:** May 2026 = 217 ADIZ incursions; June 2026 = ~214 total sorties / 134 median-line crossings (with 12 days of zero median-line crossings). Jan–May 2026 sortie totals were the lowest for their respective months since 2023. Notably, naval activity diverged upward (225 PLAN vessels in June, a record June high), even as air activity stayed low.

sources.title: China & Taiwan Update, July 2, 2026
sources.snippet: June 2026: 134 aerial ADIZ incursions across median line; 12 days with no incursions; PLA reverted to pre-Lai baseline since start of 2026.

sources.title: China & Taiwan Update, June 5, 2026
sources.snippet: May 2026: 217 aerial ADIZ incursions, slight increase from April but well below the 300+/month post-Lai average. No balloons since February 2026.

sources.title: Monthly Update on PLA Air and Maritime Maneuvers Around Taiwan (June 2026)
sources.snippet: June 2026: 134 median line crossings, 214 total sorties; Jan-May 2026 lowest for corresponding month since 2023; May had no day above 30 sorties (last was March 17); 225 PLAN vessels, record June high.

sources.title: All Quiet in the Taiwan Strait? Explaining the Recent Drop in PLA Aircraft Activity Around Taiwan
sources.snippet: From Feb 27 MND reported no PLA aircraft near ADIZ; unusually long pause; extremely rare across Eastern Theater Command.

sources.title: For nearly two weeks, Chinese fighter jets stopped buzzing Taiwan
sources.snippet: Aircraft stopped coming for nearly two weeks; spell broken with 5 PLA aircraft on Thursday (~March 12).

sources.title: March 2026 PLA aircraft sorties total and peak
sources.snippet: March 2026: 171 total sorties, lowest since Aug 2022; peak of 36 aircraft recorded on March 17, 2026.

sources.title: The Daily-Ish Update (96 hours ending 17 March 2026)
sources.snippet: Mar 14-15 detected 26 PLA aircraft, largest since Feb 25 and first clear return to large activity after the lull.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (May 17, 2026)
sources.snippet: 5 sorties of PLA aircraft detected May 16-17, 2026; 4 of 5 crossed median line.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (May 27, 2026)
sources.snippet: 10 sorties of PLA aircraft and 7 PLAN ships detected May 26-27, 2026; 9 of 10 crossed median line.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (June 26, 2026)
sources.snippet: 2 sorties of PLA aircraft and 6 PLAN ships detected June 25-26, 2026.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 6 PLA aircraft sorties, 8 PLAN vessels around territory
sources.snippet: June 13, 2026: 6 PLA aircraft sorties, 8 PLAN vessels; 4 of 6 entered southwestern ADIZ.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 22 Chinese aircraft sorties
sources.snippet: July 2, 2026: 22 sorties detected; 20 crossed median line (J-16, H-6, KJ-500).

sources.title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Flare, Chinese Fighters Reappear in Force
sources.snippet: March 15, 2026: 26 PLA aircraft detected, marking resumption after two-week absence.

sources.title: The Mysterious Dip in China's Military Flights Around Taiwan
sources.snippet: Dip coincided with China's Two Sessions; CSIS's Brian Hart called it not mysterious.
